user_story: As a Public User, I want to search for available campsites by location, date range, and amenities so that I can find a campsite that meets my trip planning needs
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am on the public portal homepage
When I enter a destination (park name, city, or zip code), select arrival/departure dates, and filter by amenities (hookups, ADA accessible, pet-friendly)
Then the system displays a map-based and list-based view of matching campsites with availability indicators and real-time pricing
And results are returned within 3 seconds for searches across 6,000+ facilities
And I can refine my search using additional filters (campsite type, group size, vehicle length)
source: SOO Section 6.6 — public portal requirements; SOO Section 6.6.3 — advanced search with maps
assumptions: Experience Cloud Digital Experiences with Lightning Web Components for search UI; integration with Mapbox or ArcGIS for 1m GSD satellite imagery; custom Apex controllers for search logic querying Campsite and Facility custom objects; ElasticSearch or Platform Search for sub-3-second response times at scale

user_story: As a Public User, I want to view detailed campsite information including photos, amenities, site-specific rules, and real-time availability calendar so that I can make an informed booking decision
acceptance_criteria:
Given I have selected a campsite from search results
When I view the campsite detail page
Then I see high-resolution photos, 360-degree views (if available), site dimensions, hookup types, accessibility features, and pet policies
And I see a 12-month availability calendar with color-coded indicators (available, booked, blocked, seasonal closure)
And pricing is displayed per night with tax/fee breakdown
And all content meets WCAG Level AA accessibility standards
source: SOO Section 6.6 — public portal requirements; Section 508/WCAG Level AA compliance
assumptions: Experience Cloud CMS for content management; Files storage for photos/media; custom Lightning Web Components for interactive calendar; Shield for PII if user-generated content is stored; accessibility testing via third-party tools (e.g., Deque axe, WAVE)

user_story: As a Public User, I want to create a secure account using Login.gov SSO so that I can manage my reservations and personal information with federated identity assurance
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am a new user visiting the public portal
When I click 'Sign In' or 'Create Account'
Then I am redirected to Login.gov for authentication via SAML 2.0 or OpenID Connect
And after successful authentication, I am returned to the portal with a session established
And my profile is automatically created in Salesforce with Login.gov UUID as the external ID
And no passwords are stored in Salesforce (federated authentication only)
source: SOO Section 6.4 — Login.gov SSO integration; FedRAMP Moderate compliance
assumptions: Experience Cloud configured for Login.gov as SSO provider (SAML 2.0 or OIDC); custom User provisioning handler (Apex Just-In-Time Provisioning); Shield encryption for PII in User/Contact records; testing with Login.gov sandbox environment

user_story: As a Public User, I want to add one or more campsites to my cart and proceed through a streamlined checkout process so that I can complete my reservation quickly on mobile or desktop
acceptance_criteria:
Given I have selected available campsites
When I add them to my cart and proceed to checkout
Then I see a cart summary with itemized pricing (nightly rate x nights, taxes, fees, total)
And I complete checkout in 3 steps: Review Cart → Guest/Vehicle Details → Payment
And the UI is mobile-optimized (responsive design, touch-friendly, <3 second page load)
And I can apply a promo code or pass discount if eligible
And accessibility is maintained (keyboard navigation, screen reader support)
source: SOO Section 6.6 — mobile-first design; Section 508/WCAG Level AA
assumptions: Experience Cloud with custom Lightning Web Components for cart/checkout flow; Apex transaction management for cart operations; integration with Business Rules engine (E04) for dynamic pricing and eligibility; consideration of Commerce Cloud for cart management (if budget allows) or custom Platform implementation

user_story: As a Public User, I want to pay for my reservation using a credit or debit card with PCI-compliant tokenization so that my payment information is secure and I receive immediate confirmation
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am on the payment step of checkout
When I enter my credit/debit card information
Then the card data is tokenized via Worldpay integration (never stored in Salesforce)
And the payment is processed in real-time with success/failure response within 5 seconds
And upon success, I receive an on-screen confirmation with reservation number and email receipt
And upon failure, I see a clear error message with retry option
And the UI complies with PCI DSS standards (no plain-text card storage, iframe tokenization)
source: SOO Section 6.7 — Worldpay integration; PCI DSS compliance
assumptions: Platform REST API callout to Worldpay tokenization endpoint; custom Apex payment processor class; Shield Platform Encryption for transaction records (not card data); retry logic and error handling for gateway timeouts; testing with Worldpay sandbox

user_story: As a Public User, I want to view my upcoming and past reservations in a dashboard so that I can manage my trips and access confirmation details
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am logged into my account
When I navigate to 'My Reservations'
Then I see separate tabs for Upcoming Reservations and Past Reservations
And each reservation displays confirmation number, campsite name, dates, location, status (confirmed, cancelled, modified), and total paid
And I can click a reservation to view full details, download a PDF confirmation, or access cancellation/modification options
And the dashboard is accessible on mobile devices with responsive design
source: SOO Section 6.6 — public portal self-service features
assumptions: Experience Cloud authenticated user portal with custom Lightning Web Components; SOQL queries on Reservation custom object filtered by authenticated user's Contact ID; PDF generation via Visualforce (legacy) or third-party API (modern); caching for performance at scale

user_story: As a Public User, I want to cancel my reservation online and receive an automated refund based on the facility's cancellation policy so that I can manage my plans without contacting customer service
acceptance_criteria:
Given I have an upcoming reservation
When I select 'Cancel Reservation' and confirm the cancellation
Then the system evaluates the facility's cancellation policy (full refund, partial refund, no refund based on days before arrival)
And I see a refund estimate before final confirmation
And upon confirmation, the reservation status is updated to 'Cancelled' and a refund transaction is initiated with Worldpay
And I receive an email confirmation with cancellation details and refund timeline
source: SOO Section 6.6 — self-service cancellation; SOO Section 6.7 — refund processing
assumptions: Apex cancellation handler that evaluates Business Rule records for cancellation windows; Platform callout to Worldpay refund API; custom metadata for cancellation policies per Facility; workflow automation (Flow or Process Builder) for status updates and email notifications; refund processing time subject to Worldpay SLA (typically 5-7 business days)

user_story: As a Public User, I want to modify my reservation (change dates, add nights, change campsite) online so that I can adjust my trip without losing my original booking
acceptance_criteria:
Given I have an upcoming reservation within the modification window
When I select 'Modify Reservation' and choose new dates or a different campsite
Then the system checks availability for the new selection and calculates any price difference
And I see a summary showing old vs. new reservation details and any additional charges or credits
And I can confirm the modification and pay any difference, or receive a refund if the new price is lower
And the modification is processed atomically (original reservation cancelled and new reservation created as a single transaction)
source: SOO Section 6.6 — reservation modification
assumptions: Apex transaction with database locks to prevent double-booking during modification; Business Rules engine (E04) evaluates modification windows; Worldpay integration for incremental charges or refunds; rollback logic if payment fails; email confirmation of modification

user_story: As a Public User, I want to search for and book recreation permits (backcountry permits, day-use permits) with trailhead quotas and group size restrictions so that I can plan permitted activities
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am searching for permits on the public portal
When I select a permit type, date range, and trailhead/entry point
Then the system displays availability based on daily quotas and group size limits
And I can book a permit by providing group details (number of people, overnight vs. day-use)
And the system validates that my group size does not exceed the permit's maximum
And I receive a digital permit confirmation with QR code for field verification
source: SOO Section 6.6 — multiple inventory types (permits); SOO Section 6.6.4 — permit quotas
assumptions: Permit custom object with quota and group size fields; Apex validation logic for quota enforcement; Lightning Web Components for permit search/booking flow; QR code generation via third-party library (e.g., ZXing) or Salesforce AppExchange app; mobile-optimized permit display

user_story: As a Public User, I want to purchase tickets for timed-entry events or tours (e.g., visitor center tours, ranger programs) so that I can secure my spot for time-sensitive activities
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am browsing events/tours on the public portal
When I select an event and available time slot
Then the system displays remaining capacity and allows me to select quantity (up to per-transaction limit)
And I can add tickets to my cart and proceed to checkout
And the system reserves my tickets during checkout (holds inventory for 10 minutes)
And I receive a digital ticket with barcode/QR code for event entry
And tickets are non-transferable unless the event policy allows it
source: SOO Section 6.6 — multiple inventory types (tickets)
assumptions: Ticket custom object with time slot and capacity fields; Apex inventory hold logic (time-bound reservation); Flow for expired holds cleanup; Experience Cloud checkout flow reuse; barcode/QR code generation; email delivery of digital tickets

user_story: As a Public User, I want to purchase an annual or season pass for a park or group of parks so that I can enjoy unlimited access and save on individual visit fees
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am on the passes purchase page
When I select a pass type (annual, seasonal, multi-park) and provide vehicle/personal information
Then the system validates eligibility (e.g., senior, military, disabled discounts) and calculates pricing
And I complete payment and receive a digital pass with unique ID and expiration date
And the pass is linked to my account for use at entry points (vehicle license plate or ID verification)
And I can view my active passes in my account dashboard
source: SOO Section 6.6 — multiple inventory types (passes)
assumptions: Pass custom object with validity period and scope (single facility, multi-facility); Apex eligibility validation (integration with external discount verification systems if required); digital pass storage in Experience Cloud authenticated portal; potential integration with field entry systems (future epic) for pass verification

user_story: As a Public User, I want to enter a lottery for high-demand permits or campsites (e.g., Half Dome permits, popular campgrounds) so that I have a fair chance to access scarce resources
acceptance_criteria:
Given a lottery is open for a high-demand permit/campsite
When I submit a lottery application with my preferred dates and alternates
Then my application is entered into the lottery system with a unique confirmation number
And I can track my application status (pending, selected, not selected) in my account
And if selected, I receive an email notification with a deadline to confirm and pay
And if I do not confirm/pay by the deadline, the selection is forfeited and offered to the next applicant
source: SOO Section 6.6 — multiple inventory types (lotteries)
assumptions: Lottery custom object with application period, draw date, and selection logic; Apex scheduled batch job for lottery draw (random selection with fairness algorithm); Flow for notification and payment deadline enforcement; Experience Cloud UI for application submission and status tracking; integration with Business Rules engine (E04) for lottery configuration

user_story: As a Public User with disabilities, I want the public portal to be fully accessible (WCAG Level AA, Section 508 compliant) so that I can independently search, book, and manage reservations using assistive technologies
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am using a screen reader (JAWS, NVDA, VoiceOver) or keyboard-only navigation
When I navigate the public portal
Then all interactive elements (buttons, links, form fields) are accessible via keyboard (Tab, Enter, Space, Arrow keys)
And all images have descriptive alt text, form fields have labels, and error messages are announced by screen readers
And color contrast meets WCAG Level AA standards (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text)
And the portal passes automated accessibility testing (axe, WAVE) and manual testing with assistive technologies
And the portal includes a 'Skip to Main Content' link for keyboard users
source: Section 508 compliance; SOO Section 6.6 — accessibility requirements
assumptions: Experience Cloud with accessible Lightning Web Components (LWC open-source components as baseline); semantic HTML and ARIA attributes; accessibility testing integrated into CI/CD pipeline; annual VPAT (Voluntary Product Accessibility Template) certification; training for developers on WCAG standards

user_story: As a Public User, I want to view reservable facilities on an interactive map with satellite imagery (1m GSD) so that I can visually explore campsite locations and surroundings before booking
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am on the search results page
When I switch to map view
Then I see an interactive map (Mapbox or ArcGIS) with pins representing available facilities
And I can toggle between street view and satellite imagery with 1m Ground Sample Distance (GSD) resolution
And clicking a pin displays a popup with facility name, availability, pricing, and 'Book Now' button
And I can zoom, pan, and draw a search area on the map to refine results
And map performance is optimized (lazy loading, clustering for dense areas)
source: SOO Section 6.6.3 — maps with 1m GSD satellite imagery
assumptions: Integration with Mapbox or ArcGIS API for mapping and satellite imagery; Lightning Web Component for map UI; Facility custom object with geolocation fields (latitude/longitude); Platform callout to map provider API; consideration of caching and CDN for map tiles; compliance with federal mapping standards if applicable

user_story: As an Agency Program Manager, I want to configure white-label widgets for my agency's website so that visitors can search and book reservations without leaving our site
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am logged into the agency portal
When I access the 'Widget Configuration' tool
Then I can customize widget appearance (colors, logo, fonts) to match my agency's branding
And I can generate embeddable JavaScript code to insert into my agency's website
And the widget displays search and booking functionality within an iframe, with deep-linking to the main portal for checkout
And transactions initiated via widget are tracked back to the source agency for reporting purposes
source: SOO Section 6.6 — white-label widgets for agency websites
assumptions: Experience Cloud with white-label configuration (custom branding per agency Community); JavaScript widget SDK with iframe embedding; Lightning Web Component for widget configuration UI in agency portal (E02); cross-origin resource sharing (CORS) configuration for secure embedding; UTM parameters or similar for tracking widget-sourced reservations

user_story: As a Public User, I want the public portal to perform well on mobile devices (iOS, Android) and slow networks so that I can book reservations from remote locations or while traveling
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am using a mobile device with 3G/4G connectivity
When I access the public portal
Then pages load within 3 seconds on average
And the UI is responsive (adapts to screen sizes 320px-1920px wide)
And images and media are optimized for mobile bandwidth (lazy loading, compression)
And I can complete search and checkout flows without horizontal scrolling or pinch-zoom
And the portal achieves a Lighthouse score of 90+ for Performance and Accessibility
source: SOO Section 6.6 — mobile-first design
assumptions: Experience Cloud with mobile-optimized Lightning Web Components; CDN for static assets (Salesforce CDN or external CDN like CloudFront); image compression and lazy loading; Progressive Web App (PWA) consideration for offline caching; performance testing with mobile emulation and real devices; monitoring via Google Analytics or similar

user_story: As a Public User, I want to receive real-time error messages and validation feedback during search and checkout so that I can correct issues immediately without losing my progress
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am filling out a form (search, checkout, profile)
When I enter invalid data (e.g., arrival date before today, invalid email format, credit card number incorrect)
Then I see an inline error message next to the field describing the issue
And the error message is accessible to screen readers
And I cannot proceed to the next step until all errors are resolved
And my valid entries are preserved (no data loss) when I correct errors
And system-level errors (e.g., payment gateway timeout) display a user-friendly message with support contact information
source: SOO Section 6.6 — user experience best practices
assumptions: Lightning Web Components with built-in validation (lightning-input, custom Apex validation); JavaScript client-side validation for immediate feedback; Apex server-side validation for security; user-friendly error messages (no stack traces or technical jargon); error logging to Platform Events or Shield Event Monitoring for debugging

user_story: As a Developer, I want the public portal to expose RESTful APIs for third-party integrations so that partner applications (mobile apps, agency tools) can search inventory and retrieve reservation data
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am a third-party developer with API credentials
When I make a REST API call to search for available campsites
Then I receive a JSON response with availability, pricing, and facility details
And the API supports filtering by location, date range, amenities, and inventory type
And the API is secured with OAuth 2.0 (client credentials or JWT bearer flow)
And API usage is rate-limited (e.g., 1000 requests/hour per client) and monitored
And API documentation is available via Swagger/OpenAPI specification
source: SOO Section 6.5.6 — RIDB open APIs (assumes similar public-facing API for reservation system)
assumptions: Platform REST API with custom Apex REST classes; API Gateway (Salesforce or external like MuleSoft) for rate limiting and monitoring; OAuth 2.0 via Salesforce Connected App; Swagger/OpenAPI documentation generated from Apex annotations or manually maintained; testing with Postman or similar; consideration of API versioning strategy

user_story: As a Public User, I want to save my favorite facilities and receive notifications when reservations open so that I can book popular campsites as soon as they become available
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am logged into my account
When I mark a facility as a favorite
Then it is saved to my account and displayed in 'My Favorites' section
And I can opt-in to receive email or SMS notifications when reservations open for that facility (based on facility's booking window)
And I receive a notification 24-48 hours before the booking window opens
And the notification includes a deep-link to the facility's reservation page
source: SOO Section 6.6 — user engagement features
assumptions: Favorite custom object (junction between Contact and Facility); Flow or Apex scheduled job to check booking window dates and trigger notifications; Marketing Cloud or Platform email/SMS for notifications; notification preferences stored in User/Contact record; consideration of Marketing Cloud integration if E10 is in scope

user_story: As a Public User, I want to search for group campsites that accommodate large parties (e.g., 20+ people, multiple vehicles) so that I can plan group trips with family or organizations
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am searching for campsites
When I filter by 'Group Campsite' and specify group size and number of vehicles
Then the system returns only campsites that meet the minimum capacity requirements
And I see group pricing (flat rate per night or per-person pricing)
And I can view group-specific amenities (fire rings, pavilions, parking areas)
And I can book the group campsite through the standard checkout flow
source: SOO Section 6.6 — multiple inventory types (group campsites)
assumptions: Campsite custom object with group capacity and pricing fields; Apex search logic for group size filtering; Lightning Web Component UI for group search; Business Rules engine (E04) for group-specific booking windows and restrictions; consideration of group permit requirements (may link to E04)

user_story: As a Public User, I want to view current conditions and alerts for facilities (weather, road closures, fire bans) so that I can plan my trip with the most up-to-date information
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am viewing a facility detail page
When current conditions or alerts are active
Then I see a prominent alert banner with the message and severity level (info, warning, critical)
And alerts are sourced from RIDB data feeds or agency CMS integrations
And I can view historical alerts and conditions (e.g., seasonal closure patterns)
And alerts are accessible to screen readers and mobile users
source: SOO Section 6.5.6 — RIDB integration; SOO Section 6.6 — facility information display
assumptions: Alert custom object or Data Cloud entity for conditions/alerts; integration with agency CMS or external weather/conditions APIs; Flow or Apex trigger to display alerts on Facility pages; Lightning Web Component for alert banner; consideration of push notifications for users with active reservations at affected facilities

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to monitor public portal performance (page load times, search latency, checkout conversion rates) via dashboards so that I can proactively identify and resolve performance bottlenecks
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am logged into the Salesforce admin console
When I navigate to the 'Portal Performance' dashboard
Then I see real-time metrics for page load times, API response times, search query performance, and checkout funnel drop-off rates
And I can filter metrics by time range (last hour, day, week, month)
And I can drill down into specific performance issues (e.g., slow search queries, payment gateway timeouts)
And the dashboard triggers alerts when metrics exceed thresholds (e.g., >5 second page load, >10% checkout abandonment)
source: SOO Section 6.5 — performance monitoring; SOO Section 6.7.8 — reporting requirements
assumptions: CRM Analytics (Tableau) dashboard for portal metrics; Platform Event Monitoring or custom logging (Apex, Lightning Web Components) for performance data collection; integration with external monitoring tools (e.g., Google Analytics, New Relic) if required; Shield Event Monitoring for detailed transaction logs; alert automation via Flow or Apex scheduled jobs

user_story: As a Public User, I want to print or download a PDF confirmation of my reservation so that I can present it at the facility entrance or keep it for my records
acceptance_criteria:
Given I have completed a reservation
When I view my reservation details in 'My Reservations'
Then I see a 'Download PDF' or 'Print Confirmation' button
And clicking it generates a PDF with reservation details (confirmation number, dates, facility, site, guest details, QR code for field verification)
And the PDF is formatted for standard US Letter (8.5x11) or A4 paper
And the PDF is accessible (tagged PDF for screen readers)
source: SOO Section 6.6 — reservation confirmation features
assumptions: Visualforce PDF renderer (legacy) or third-party PDF generation API (e.g., Conga, Drawloop, or open-source library); Apex controller to populate PDF template with reservation data; QR code generation for field verification; consideration of multilingual support if required; PDF stored in Files and linked to Reservation record

user_story: As a Public User, I want to receive automated email confirmations at key points in my reservation lifecycle (booking, modification, cancellation, 7-day reminder) so that I have timely updates on my reservation status
acceptance_criteria:
Given I have completed a booking, modification, or cancellation
When the transaction is finalized
Then I receive an email confirmation within 1 minute
And the email includes reservation details, confirmation number, cancellation policy, and a link to view/manage my reservation
And 7 days before my arrival date, I receive a reminder email with campsite details, directions, and check-in instructions
And all emails are mobile-friendly and accessible (plain-text alternative, descriptive links)
source: SOO Section 6.6 — customer communication; SOO Section 6.8 — email notifications
assumptions: Platform email templates (Classic Email Templates or Lightning Email Templates); Flow or Apex trigger for email automation; scheduled Flow or Apex batch job for 7-day reminders; email deliverability via Salesforce relay or external SMTP (if required); consideration of Marketing Cloud for advanced email features (if E10 is in scope); unsubscribe/opt-out compliance (CAN-SPAM Act)

user_story: As a Contact Center Agent, I want to access the public portal on behalf of a customer to troubleshoot issues, complete bookings, or provide assistance so that I can deliver seamless customer support
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am a Contact Center Agent logged into Service Cloud
When I receive a call from a customer with a reservation issue
Then I can search for the customer's reservation by confirmation number, email, or phone number
And I can view the customer's perspective of the public portal (via embedded iframe or separate agent view)
And I can complete actions on behalf of the customer (modify, cancel, re-send confirmation) with proper audit logging
And all actions are recorded in the case history for accountability
source: SOO Section 6.6.12 — contact center integration with public portal
assumptions: Service Cloud Console integration with Experience Cloud; Lightning Web Component or iframe embedding of public portal in Service Cloud; agent authentication with elevated permissions (Profile or Permission Set); Platform Event logging for agent actions; consideration of Service Cloud Voice integration (E03) for call context; training for agents on portal navigation

user_story: As an Agency Program Manager, I want to access a role-based partner portal so that I can manage reservations and inventory for facilities under my jurisdiction without requiring full system admin access
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am an authenticated Agency Program Manager
When I log into the partner portal via Login.gov SSO
Then I see only facilities and data scoped to my agency and assigned regions
And I can navigate to inventory management, reporting, and configuration tools
And unauthorized features are hidden from the navigation
source: SOO Section 6.6 — back-office tools for 5,000 stakeholders
assumptions: Experience Cloud Partner Community with custom profile and permission sets; Login.gov SSO integration for government users; row-level security via sharing rules based on Agency hierarchy

user_story: As a Field Staff member at a remote campground, I want to use an offline-capable mobile app so that I can check in guests, view reservations, and update inventory status even when cellular/Wi-Fi connectivity is unavailable
acceptance_criteria:
Given I have the Salesforce Mobile SDK app installed on my device
When I lose internet connectivity at a remote site
Then the app continues to function using locally cached data for my assigned facility
And I can perform check-ins, cancellations, and status updates offline
And changes sync automatically when connectivity is restored
And conflicts are detected and flagged for manual resolution
source: SOO Section 6.6 — offline-capable mobile app for field users at remote sites
assumptions: Salesforce Mobile SDK with Smartstore for local persistence; sync logic via Platform APIs; conflict resolution workflow for offline changes

user_story: As a Field Staff member, I want to search for and view current and upcoming reservations at my facility so that I can prepare for guest arrivals and manage check-ins efficiently
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am logged into the partner portal
When I navigate to the reservation search page
Then I can filter by date range, guest name, reservation ID, or campsite number
And I see a list of matching reservations with status, guest details, and check-in/out dates
And I can drill into reservation details including payment status and special requests
source: SOO Section 6.6 — inventory management and reservation tools
assumptions: Custom Lightning Web Components on Experience Cloud; search built on SOSL or List Views with dynamic filters

user_story: As a Field Staff member, I want to manually check in a guest who arrives at my facility so that the reservation status is updated in real time and the campsite is marked as occupied
acceptance_criteria:
Given I have a confirmed reservation in the system
When I select the reservation and click 'Check In'
Then the reservation status changes from 'Confirmed' to 'Checked In'
And the campsite availability is updated to 'Occupied'
And a timestamp and user ID are recorded for audit purposes
And the guest receives a confirmation notification (if enabled)
source: SOO Section 6.6 — field user check-in workflows
assumptions: Custom Flow or Apex for check-in logic; Platform Events for real-time availability updates; integration with notification service (Marketing Cloud or Experience Cloud email)

user_story: As an Agency Program Manager, I want to override or cancel a reservation with an audit trail so that I can handle emergency situations or policy violations while maintaining accountability
acceptance_criteria:
Given I have the appropriate permissions
When I select a reservation and choose 'Override' or 'Cancel'
Then I am prompted to enter a reason code and notes
And the system creates an audit log record with timestamp, user, reason, and previous state
And the guest is notified of the cancellation and refund process (if applicable)
And the campsite becomes available for rebooking
source: SOO Section 6.6 — reservation overrides with audit trail
assumptions: Custom Lightning component for override UI; Field History Tracking and Platform Event Monitoring for audit trail; refund workflow integration with payment processor

user_story: As an Agency Program Manager, I want to configure business rules for my facilities (booking windows, cut-off times, max stay) so that the public portal enforces agency-specific policies automatically
acceptance_criteria:
Given I navigate to the business rule configuration page for a facility
When I set values for booking window (days ahead), cut-off window (hours before), and max length of stay
Then the system validates inputs (e.g., max stay cannot exceed season length)
And changes are saved with effective dates and version history
And the public portal immediately reflects the new rules for future bookings
And existing reservations are grandfathered under previous rules
source: SOO Section 6.6 — business rule configuration; Sample-Draft-BusinesRules.xlsx
assumptions: Custom metadata types or custom objects for business rules; versioning via effective date fields; Apex triggers enforce rules at booking time

user_story: As a Field Staff member, I want to mark a campsite as temporarily unavailable (maintenance, hazard) so that the public cannot book it until the issue is resolved
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am viewing campsite inventory for my facility
When I select a campsite and choose 'Mark Unavailable'
Then I am prompted for a reason (Maintenance, Hazard, Other) and optional notes
And the campsite status changes to 'Unavailable'
And the campsite is removed from public search results and booking calendars
And I can later mark it 'Available' to restore public booking
source: SOO Section 6.6 — inventory management at field level
assumptions: Campsite custom object with Status picklist; real-time sync to public portal via Platform Events or polling; audit log for status changes

user_story: As an Agency Program Manager, I want to generate a utilization report for my facilities so that I can analyze occupancy rates, revenue trends, and optimize inventory allocation
acceptance_criteria:
Given I navigate to the reporting dashboard
When I select 'Utilization Report' and specify date range and facilities
Then the system generates a report showing occupancy percentage, total revenue, cancellations, and no-shows
And the report can be exported to CSV or Excel
And I can drill down by facility, campsite type, and time period
source: SOO Section 6.6 — reporting for agency stakeholders
assumptions: CRM Analytics (Tableau) embedded in Experience Cloud; pre-built report templates with drill-down; export via Tableau APIs

user_story: As a Concessionaire managing reservations on behalf of an agency, I want role-based access to only the facilities I operate so that I can manage inventory and reservations without seeing other agencies' data
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am a Concessionaire user with assigned facilities
When I log into the partner portal
Then I see only the facilities I am contracted to operate
And I can manage reservations, inventory, and business rules for those facilities
And I cannot view or modify data for facilities outside my scope
And my actions are logged with my Concessionaire organization ID
source: SOO Section 6.6 — concessionaire access to partner portal
assumptions: Experience Cloud External User license; Account hierarchy for Concessionaires; sharing rules and permission sets scoped by Facility ownership

user_story: As a Field Staff member, I want to process a walk-up reservation for a guest who arrives without a prior booking so that I can capture the reservation, collect payment, and update inventory in real time
acceptance_criteria:
Given an available campsite exists at my facility
When I create a new 'Walk-Up Reservation' and enter guest details, dates, and payment method
Then the system validates availability and business rules (max stay, cut-off)
And the reservation is created with status 'Checked In'
And the campsite is marked as occupied
And payment is processed via integrated terminal or recorded for later reconciliation
source: SOO Section 6.6 — field user walk-up reservation capability
assumptions: Custom Flow or Lightning component for walk-up bookings; integration with Worldpay/EMV terminal via API; fallback for offline payment recording

user_story: As an Agency Program Manager, I want to bulk import facility and campsite inventory from a CSV file so that I can onboard new sites or update existing inventory efficiently
acceptance_criteria:
Given I have a CSV file with facility and campsite data in the required format
When I navigate to the bulk import tool and upload the file
Then the system validates the data for required fields, duplicates, and format errors
And I see a preview of records to be created or updated
And I confirm the import, and the system creates/updates records in batch

user_story: As a Developer, I want to register my application in the RIDB developer portal and obtain API credentials (OAuth 2.0 tokens) so that I can authenticate and make authorized API calls to access federal recreation data
acceptance_criteria:

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to monitor RIDB API usage and performance metrics (request volume, latency, error rates) so that I can ensure SLA compliance and identify performance bottlenecks or abuse patterns

user_story: As Field Staff at a remote facility with limited connectivity, I want RIDB facility data to be cached locally in the Salesforce Mobile app so that I can access site information and provide visitor assistance even when offline

user_story: As a Public User, I want to view detailed facility information from RIDB (photos, descriptions, amenities, operating hours, fees, contact info) on the facility detail page so that I can make an informed decision before booking or visiting

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to configure data retention policies for RIDB historical records so that we comply with federal records management requirements while optimizing Data Cloud storage costs

user_story: As a Security Administrator, I want to enforce FedRAMP Moderate security controls on RIDB data at rest and in transit so that we maintain compliance with federal security requirements and protect sensitive facility information

assumptions: Salesforce Government Cloud with FedRAMP Moderate pre-authorization; Shield Platform Encryption enabled for Data Cloud objects and custom fields; Shield Platform Event Monitoring captures all data access events; Named Credentials for secure API credential management; Zero Trust architecture with MFA for admin access

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to configure rate limiting and DDoS protection for the public RIDB API so that we prevent abuse and ensure fair access for all developers while maintaining system availability
acceptance_criteria:

user_story: As a Contact Center Agent, I want to search RIDB within Service Cloud console while on a call so that I can quickly answer customer questions about non-reservable recreation sites (trails, visitor centers, picnic areas) without leaving my workspace

user_story: As a Public User, I want to authenticate using Login.gov SSO so that I can securely access my Recreation.gov account without managing another password

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to enable Shield Platform Encryption for all PII fields so that customer data is encrypted at rest and meets FedRAMP data protection requirements

acceptance_criteria:
Given a Full Sandbox with Shield and Event Monitoring enabled
When I deploy new custom objects, Apex classes, or integrations
Then I can test Shield Platform Encryption on PII fields and verify encrypted data is searchable
And I can test role-based access controls and verify unauthorized users cannot access restricted records
And I can test Event Monitoring and verify authentication, API, and data access events are logged
And I can test Transaction Security policies and verify high-risk actions are blocked or alerted
And sandbox security configuration mirrors production to ensure accurate testing
source: SOO Section 6.4 - Zero Trust architecture requires security testing
assumptions: Full Sandbox includes Shield and Event Monitoring for pre-production testing; sandbox security config (profiles, permission sets, encryption) matches production; security testing included in UAT and regression test plans

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to implement break-glass emergency access procedures so that authorized administrators can access the system during a security incident or outage
acceptance_criteria:
Given a security incident or system outage occurs
When an authorized administrator (e.g., USDA ISSO, SI lead) needs emergency access
Then a break-glass account is available with System Administrator profile and all object access
And break-glass account credentials are stored in a secure vault (e.g., CyberArk, physical safe)
And break-glass account usage requires approval from 2+ authorized officials
And all break-glass login and actions are logged in Event Monitoring with audit trail
And break-glass access is reviewed post-incident and credentials are rotated after each use
source: SOO Section 6.4 - Zero Trust with emergency access controls per AC-2(7)
assumptions: Break-glass accounts configured with System Administrator profile; MFA exempt but logged extensively; credentials stored in secure vault and rotated after use; usage documented in security incident report

user_story: As a Public User, I want to receive a notification when my account security settings change so that I can detect unauthorized account modifications
acceptance_criteria:
Given my Recreation.gov account is active
When my password, email, phone, or payment method is updated
Then I receive an email and/or SMS notification confirming the change
And the notification includes timestamp, IP address, and device information
And the notification includes a link to report unauthorized changes
And if I report unauthorized changes, my account is automatically locked pending investigation
And security notification preferences are configurable in my account settings
source: SOO Section 6.4 - Zero Trust with user security awareness
assumptions: Experience Cloud triggers notifications via Process Builder or Flow when Person Account fields change; notifications sent via Email-to-Case or Marketing Cloud; unauthorized change reporting creates Case for security team review

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to enforce data residency requirements so that all customer data remains within FedRAMP-authorized US data centers
acceptance_criteria:
Given the system is deployed on Salesforce Government Cloud
When customer data is stored or processed
Then all data resides exclusively in US-based data centers (Virginia, Illinois, or Oregon)
And no data is replicated or backed up outside the United States
And third-party integrations (Worldpay, Mapbox, ArcGIS) also comply with US data residency
And data residency compliance is documented in System Security Plan
And data residency is verified via Salesforce Trust dashboard and contractual agreements
source: SOO Section 6.4 - FedRAMP Moderate requires US data residency
assumptions: Government Cloud enforces US data residency per FedRAMP requirements; no data replication to international data centers; third-party vendors selected based on US data residency commitment; Trust dashboard provides data center location transparency

user_story: As a Compliance Manager, I want to coordinate with Salesforce for annual FedRAMP audits so that Government Cloud maintains its authorization and Recreation.gov inherits compliance
acceptance_criteria:
Given Recreation.gov relies on Salesforce Government Cloud FedRAMP authorization
When Salesforce conducts annual FedRAMP audit with 3PAO
Then I coordinate with Salesforce to provide application-level documentation (SSP, POA&M, incident reports)
And I participate in 3PAO interviews and provide evidence for application-level controls
And any audit findings affecting Recreation.gov are addressed within required timeframes
And Government Cloud maintains continuous FedRAMP Moderate authorization
And Recreation.gov ATO remains valid as long as Government Cloud authorization is active
source: SOO Section 6.4 - FedRAMP compliance via Government Cloud inheritance
assumptions: Government Cloud FedRAMP authorization covers infrastructure and platform controls (IaaS/PaaS); Recreation.gov responsible for application-level controls (access management, secure code, event monitoring); annual 3PAO audit coordinated with Salesforce FedRAMP team

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to assess and profile the 21M+ historical reservation records in the incumbent system so that I can design an accurate data migration strategy and identify data quality issues before migration
acceptance_criteria:
Given access to the incumbent system database
When I execute data profiling queries and analysis
Then I receive a comprehensive data quality report showing record counts, field completeness, duplicate rates, and data anomalies
And I can identify required data transformations and cleansing rules
And I document data mapping specifications from source to Salesforce target schema
source: SOO Section 6.3 — transition-in requirements, data migration planning
assumptions: Data Loader and external ETL tool (MuleSoft or Heroku) used for bulk data migration. Access to incumbent contractor's system per transition-out requirements.

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to migrate 6,000+ facility inventory definitions and their business rules first so that I have the foundational master data in place before migrating transactional reservation records
acceptance_criteria:
Given cleaned and validated facility inventory data from the incumbent system
When I execute the Phase 1 migration using Data Loader and Bulk API 2.0
Then all 6,000+ facilities are created in Salesforce with correct attributes (name, location, capacity, amenities)
And business rules (booking windows, cut-off windows, seasons, length of stay) are properly configured per facility
And migration logs confirm 100% success rate with zero data loss
And post-migration validation confirms data integrity against source system
source: SOO Section 6.3 — phased migration approach (Inventory → Active Reservations → Historical Data → User Profiles)
assumptions: Platform Bulk API 2.0 used for large volume migrations. Custom objects for Facility, Business Rule, Season configured prior to migration.

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to migrate active reservations (future-dated bookings) in Phase 2 so that customers can continue to access their existing reservations in the new system without disruption
acceptance_criteria:
Given facility inventory successfully migrated in Phase 1
When I execute Phase 2 migration for all active/future reservations
Then all active reservations are created with correct customer links, facility links, dates, and payment status
And reservation confirmation numbers are preserved or mapped to new IDs with cross-reference maintained
And customers can immediately view their reservations in the new public portal
And no double-bookings or inventory conflicts are introduced
source: SOO Section 6.3 — phased migration, parallel operations for validation
assumptions: Active reservations migrated before go-live to ensure continuity. External ETL tool handles complex transformations and lookups.

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to migrate 21M+ historical reservation records in batched phases so that I can provide historical data for reporting and analytics without impacting system performance during go-live
acceptance_criteria:
Given active reservations and inventory successfully migrated
When I execute Phase 3 historical data migration in batches (e.g., 1M records per batch)
Then all 21M+ historical records are loaded into Salesforce within agreed timeline
And historical data is available for CRM Analytics reporting and trend analysis
And bulk API governor limits are not exceeded during migration
And migration can be paused/resumed without data corruption
source: SOO Section 6.3 — historical data migration, 21M+ records
assumptions: Platform Bulk API 2.0 with parallel processing. Historical data migrated post-go-live to prioritize operational data. Data archiving strategy defined for records older than 7 years.

user_story: As a Public User, I want to migrate my user profile and account history from the old system to Salesforce so that I can log in with my existing credentials and view my past reservations without re-registering
acceptance_criteria:
Given 10M+ user profiles in the incumbent system
When Phase 4 user profile migration executes
Then my user profile is created in Experience Cloud with my email, name, preferences, and account history
And my Login.gov SSO identity is properly linked for authentication
And I can view all my historical and active reservations in one place
And my saved favorite facilities and notification preferences are preserved
And PII is encrypted using Shield per FedRAMP requirements
source: SOO Section 6.3 — user profile migration (10M+ profiles), SOO Section 6.4 — Login.gov SSO integration
assumptions: Experience Cloud Person Accounts used for public user profiles. Shield Platform Encryption enabled for PII. Login.gov integration completed before user migration.

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to run parallel operations with both incumbent and new Salesforce systems so that I can validate data accuracy and system behavior before full cutover
acceptance_criteria:
Given both systems are operational and receiving live data
When I execute parallel operations for a defined validation period (e.g., 30 days)
Then new reservations are created in both systems simultaneously
And I can compare data consistency between systems on a daily basis
And discrepancies are logged and investigated
And I produce a parallel operations validation report confirming data accuracy > 99.9%
And stakeholders approve cutover based on validation results
source: SOO Section 6.3 — parallel operations period for validation
assumptions: MuleSoft or Heroku integration platform used to sync data between systems during parallel operations. Read-only mode enabled on incumbent system post-cutover.

user_story: As an Agency Program Manager, I want to review and validate migrated facility inventory and business rules for my facilities so that I can confirm accuracy before the system goes live
acceptance_criteria:
Given facility data has been migrated to Salesforce
When I log into the Agency Portal and navigate to my assigned facilities
Then I can view all facility attributes, amenities, and configurations
And I can verify business rules (booking windows, seasons, cut-off times) match current operational settings
And I can submit corrections or updates through a validation workflow
And my approval or feedback is tracked in the migration audit log
source: SOO Section 6.6 — Agency Program Manager portal, SOO Section 6.3 — migration validation
assumptions: Experience Cloud partner portal with role-based access for 5,000 agency stakeholders. UAT process includes data validation phase.

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to use MuleSoft/Heroku ETL tools for complex data transformations during migration so that I can handle business logic, lookups, and data cleansing beyond Data Loader capabilities
acceptance_criteria:
Given complex migration requirements (e.g., multi-table joins, conditional logic, external API calls)
When I configure ETL pipelines in MuleSoft or Heroku
Then I can extract data from incumbent system APIs or database exports
And I can apply transformations (data cleansing, format conversion, enrichment, deduplication)
And I can load data into Salesforce via Bulk API with error handling and retry logic
And I can monitor ETL job execution, performance, and failure rates in real-time
source: SOO Section 6.3 — external ETL tool (MuleSoft or Heroku) for migration
assumptions: MuleSoft or Heroku selected as ETL platform based on existing enterprise licensing and IT standards. Platform Bulk API 2.0 used for high-volume loads.

user_story: As a Developer, I want to design and implement a rollback strategy for each migration phase so that we can revert changes if critical issues are discovered post-migration
acceptance_criteria:
Given migration phases are planned with rollback contingencies
When I design the rollback procedure for each phase
Then I document rollback steps, data deletion scripts, and restoration procedures
And I create automated rollback scripts that can execute within agreed RTO (e.g., 4 hours)
And I test rollback procedures in sandbox environments to validate effectiveness
And rollback decision criteria and authorization workflows are defined and approved
source: SOO Section 6.3 — migration risk management, transition planning
assumptions: Sandbox environments used for migration testing. Backup/restore capabilities using Data Loader export and Bulk API delete operations. Critical data retained in incumbent system until validation complete.

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to migrate payment transaction history and financial reconciliation data so that complete financial audit trails are available in the new system
acceptance_criteria:
Given payment transaction records from Worldpay and US Bank Lockbox
When I migrate financial data linked to historical reservations
Then all payment records are loaded with transaction IDs, amounts, dates, payment methods, and reconciliation status
And tokenized payment information complies with PCI DSS requirements (no raw card data migrated)
And financial records are linked to correct reservation and customer records
And historical financial reports can be regenerated from migrated data
source: SOO Section 6.7 — payment processing, financial reconciliation requirements
assumptions: Financial data migrated as read-only historical records. Tokenization maintained from source system or re-tokenized via Worldpay API. Shield encryption applied to financial records.

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to establish data quality gates and validation checkpoints for each migration phase so that only clean, accurate data is loaded into Salesforce
acceptance_criteria:
Given data quality rules defined for each entity type (facilities, reservations, users)
When I execute pre-migration validation scripts
Then data quality issues (missing required fields, invalid formats, referential integrity violations) are identified and logged
And only records passing validation rules are allowed to proceed to migration
And rejected records are flagged for manual review and correction
And data quality metrics (completeness, accuracy, consistency) are reported for each batch
source: SOO Section 6.3 — data migration, quality assurance
assumptions: Platform validation rules, duplicate rules, and custom Apex validation logic used for data quality enforcement. Pre-migration validation performed in staging environment.

user_story: As a Contact Center Agent, I want to access complete customer history (including pre-migration data) in Service Cloud so that I can provide informed support without asking customers to repeat their history
acceptance_criteria:
Given historical reservations and user profiles have been migrated
When I open a customer case in Service Cloud
Then I can view the customer's complete reservation history from the incumbent system and new system in a unified timeline
And I can access notes, support tickets, and interaction history from the old system
And I can search for customers by old confirmation numbers or new IDs
And data is presented in a consistent format regardless of source system
source: SOO Section 6.6.12 — contact center operations, customer service requirements
assumptions: Service Cloud case management integrated with migrated customer and reservation data. Historical support tickets migrated as read-only records or attached as notes.

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to coordinate with the incumbent contractor for data extracts and system access so that I can obtain complete, accurate source data per SOO transition-out requirements
acceptance_criteria:
Given SOO Section 6.3 mandates incumbent contractor cooperation
When I request data extracts and documentation from the incumbent contractor
Then I receive complete database exports, API access credentials, and data dictionaries within agreed timelines
And incumbent contractor provides technical support for data mapping questions
And all data transfers are secured with encryption and audit logs
And data formats are documented and machine-readable (CSV, JSON, or database dump)
source: SOO Section 6.3 — incumbent contractor transition-out requirements
assumptions: Incumbent contractor contractually obligated to provide data and cooperation. Secure file transfer mechanism (SFTP or encrypted cloud storage) used for data exchange.

user_story: As Field Staff, I want to verify that my facility's operational data (check-in status, special conditions, maintenance schedules) is accurately migrated so that I can continue field operations without disruption
acceptance_criteria:
Given operational data has been migrated for my assigned facility
When I access the mobile app (Salesforce Mobile SDK) in the field
Then I can view current check-in status, occupancy, and maintenance schedules matching pre-migration state
And I can verify that special operational notes and alerts are preserved
And I can report discrepancies through the mobile app for immediate correction
And offline-cached data reflects the latest migrated state
source: SOO Section 6.6 — field user tools, mobile app requirements
assumptions: Salesforce Mobile SDK with offline capability used for field operations. Operational state data migrated close to cutover to minimize drift.

user_story: As a Developer, I want to build automated data migration scripts with error handling and logging so that large-volume migrations can run unattended and issues can be quickly diagnosed
acceptance_criteria:
Given migration scripts for each data entity and phase
When I execute automated migration jobs
Then scripts handle API errors, governor limits, and network failures gracefully with retry logic
And detailed logs capture record-level success/failure with error messages
And failed records are written to error files for manual review
And job status, progress, and performance metrics are available via monitoring dashboard
And scripts can resume from last successful checkpoint after interruption
source: SOO Section 6.3 — data migration, automation requirements
assumptions: Scripts built using Python/Node.js with Salesforce API libraries. Bulk API 2.0 used for parallel processing and governor limit efficiency. CloudWatch or Splunk used for log aggregation.

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to migrate lottery reservation records and results so that historical lottery data is available for reporting and public users can view their past lottery entries
acceptance_criteria:
Given lottery system data from the incumbent system (entries, draws, winners, waitlists)
When I migrate lottery records as part of Phase 3 historical data migration
Then all lottery entries are loaded with correct dates, facility links, applicant info, and outcome (won/lost/waitlist)
And lottery draw results and winner notifications are preserved
And public users can view their historical lottery entries and outcomes in their account
And lottery statistics are available for agency reporting and analysis
source: SOO Section 6.6 — lottery system requirements, historical data migration
assumptions: Custom Lottery objects configured in Salesforce Platform. Lottery data is historical read-only after migration (active lotteries managed in new system).

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to migrate concessionaire and partner organization data so that their access and inventory relationships are maintained in the new system
acceptance_criteria:
Given concessionaire and partner org data in the incumbent system
When I migrate partner organization records
Then all concessionaire accounts are created in Salesforce with correct contact info, contract details, and facility assignments
And concessionaire portal access is configured in Experience Cloud with appropriate roles/permissions
And inventory managed by concessionaires is correctly linked to their accounts
And financial arrangements and revenue-sharing agreements are documented in the system
source: SOO Section 6.6 — partner communities for concessionaires, inventory management
assumptions: Experience Cloud partner community licenses assigned to concessionaires. Account hierarchy used to model agency-concessionaire-facility relationships.

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to implement Shield Platform Encryption for all migrated PII data so that customer privacy and FedRAMP compliance requirements are met from day one
acceptance_criteria:
Given Shield Platform Encryption is configured in Government Cloud
When PII data (customer names, emails, phone numbers, addresses, transaction history) is migrated
Then all PII fields are encrypted at rest using Shield encryption
And encryption keys are managed according to FedRAMP Moderate requirements
And authorized users can decrypt and view PII based on permission sets
And audit logs track all access to encrypted PII fields
And backup/restore processes maintain encryption integrity
source: SOO Section 6.4 — FedRAMP Moderate, SOO Section 6.5 — Shield encryption for PII
assumptions: Salesforce Government Cloud with Shield Platform Encryption enabled. Key management follows NIST standards for FedRAMP.

user_story: As an Agency Program Manager, I want to maintain access to historical reports and analytics after migration so that I can compare pre- and post-migration performance trends
acceptance_criteria:
Given historical data has been migrated to Salesforce
When I access CRM Analytics (Tableau) dashboards
Then I can view historical performance metrics (utilization rates, revenue, cancellations) from the incumbent system
And I can generate comparison reports showing trends before and after cutover
And report filters and dimensions work consistently across historical and new data
And historical report definitions from the old system are replicated or replaced with equivalent dashboards
source: SOO Section 6.7.8 — reporting requirements, analytics
assumptions: CRM Analytics (Tableau) used for reporting. Historical data loaded into Data Cloud or directly into Analytics datasets. Dataflows configured to combine historical and new operational data.

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to migrate RIDB (Recreation Information Database) content and metadata so that public-facing recreation data is complete and up-to-date at go-live
acceptance_criteria:
Given RIDB data feeds from agency Content Management Systems
When I migrate RIDB content into Data Cloud
Then all recreation facilities (reservable and non-reservable) are loaded with attributes, descriptions, photos, and location coordinates
And RIDB public APIs provide access to complete dataset
And public portal search and map displays show RIDB data accurately
And data ingestion pipelines are configured for ongoing updates post-migration
source: SOO Section 6.5.6-6.5.8 — RIDB requirements, Data Cloud aggregation
assumptions: Data Cloud used as RIDB aggregation hub. Automated feeds from agency CMS systems configured. RIDB APIs built on Data Cloud APIs.

user_story: As a Developer, I want to implement incremental/delta migration capabilities so that data changes in the incumbent system during migration can be captured and loaded without full re-migration
acceptance_criteria:
Given migration occurs over several weeks with ongoing operations
When I configure delta migration logic
Then I can identify records created or updated in the source system since last migration batch
And I can load only new/changed records without re-processing the entire dataset
And delta loads merge correctly with previously migrated data (updates vs. inserts)
And conflict resolution rules handle cases where same record changed in both systems during parallel operations
source: SOO Section 6.3 — phased migration, parallel operations
assumptions: Source system provides change tracking (timestamps, change logs) for delta identification. ETL tool performs upsert operations based on external IDs or unique keys.

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to migrate seasonal configuration data and calendar settings for all facilities so that booking windows and availability are correctly calculated from day one
acceptance_criteria:
Given seasonal configurations from the incumbent system
When I migrate season records linked to facilities
Then all seasonal date ranges, operating hours, and capacity adjustments are loaded correctly
And booking window calculations (e.g., 6 months in advance from open date) produce correct availability
And seasonal rules are applied consistently across all reservation types (campsites, permits, tickets)
And I can verify seasonal logic by simulating reservations in test environment before go-live
source: SOO Section 6.6 — reservation engine, 5. Sample-Draft-BusinessRules.xlsx — seasonal configurations
assumptions: Custom Season object with relationship to Facility. Apex or Flow logic calculates booking windows based on seasonal configurations.

user_story: As a Public User, I want to be notified when my account and reservation history has been migrated so that I know I can access the new system with my existing credentials
acceptance_criteria:
Given my user profile has been migrated to Salesforce
When the migration completes and the new system goes live
Then I receive an email notification with instructions to access the new portal
And the email includes my Login.gov authentication instructions and link to the new site
And I can log in and immediately see my active and historical reservations
And I am informed of any new features or differences in the new system
source: SOO Section 6.8 — customer communications, user notifications
assumptions: Email communication sent via Marketing Cloud or Experience Cloud native email templates. Notification timing coordinated with go-live cutover.

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to establish data retention and archiving policies for historical data so that Salesforce storage limits are managed and compliance requirements are met
acceptance_criteria:
Given 21M+ historical records being migrated
When I define data retention policies
Then I identify records eligible for archiving based on age (e.g., >7 years) and business requirements
And I configure archiving workflows to move aged records to external storage (e.g., Big Objects, external data lakes)
And archived data remains accessible for compliance audits and legal holds
And storage consumption is monitored and optimized within Salesforce license limits
source: SOO Section 6.3 — data migration, storage management
assumptions: Big Objects or external data lake (Heroku Postgres, S3 via Data Cloud) used for long-term archiving. Data retention policies align with federal records management requirements (NARA guidelines).

user_story: As a Developer, I want to migrate and preserve external system integration configurations so that connections to payment processors, mapping services, and agency systems are maintained post-cutover
acceptance_criteria:
Given integration configurations in the incumbent system (APIs, webhooks, credentials)
When I document and migrate integration settings
Then all external system connections (Worldpay, US Bank, Mapbox, ArcGIS, agency CMS) are configured in Salesforce
And API keys, OAuth tokens, and service endpoints are securely stored in Named Credentials or custom settings
And integration testing confirms connectivity and data flow with all external systems
And integration monitoring and alerting are configured to detect failures post-migration
source: SOO Section 6.7 — payment integration, SOO Section 6.5.6 — RIDB feeds, mapping services
assumptions: Named Credentials used for secure credential storage. MuleSoft or Heroku used for complex integrations. Pre-cutover integration testing performed in sandbox.

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to perform a final cutover dress rehearsal in a production-like environment so that we can validate the complete migration process and timeline before the actual go-live
acceptance_criteria:
Given a full sandbox copy of production data
When I execute the complete migration process (all phases) in the dress rehearsal environment
Then all migration scripts, ETL jobs, and validation checkpoints execute successfully
And cutover timing is measured and documented (total duration, phase durations, downtime window)
And issues identified in dress rehearsal are resolved before production cutover
And dress rehearsal results are reviewed and approved by stakeholders
And go/no-go decision criteria are evaluated based on dress rehearsal outcomes
source: SOO Section 6.3 — migration planning, risk mitigation
assumptions: Full sandbox or partial copy sandbox used for dress rehearsal. Dress rehearsal performed 1-2 weeks before production cutover. Lessons learned incorporated into final runbook.

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to create detailed cutover runbooks and checklists so that the production migration executes smoothly with clear roles, responsibilities, and timing
acceptance_criteria:
Given migration phases and tasks are defined
When I create the cutover runbook
Then the runbook documents every migration step with assigned owners, estimated durations, and dependencies
And pre-cutover, cutover, and post-cutover checklists are provided for each team member
And communication protocols (status updates, escalation paths, go/no-go checkpoints) are defined
And rollback triggers and procedures are clearly documented
And the runbook is reviewed and approved by technical and business stakeholders
source: SOO Section 6.3 — transition planning, cutover execution
assumptions: Runbook maintained as living document and updated based on dress rehearsal learnings. Cutover scheduled during low-traffic period with extended customer communication.

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to configure Platform Event Monitoring and audit logs for all migration activities so that data lineage, access, and changes are tracked for compliance and troubleshooting
acceptance_criteria:
Given Platform Event Monitoring is enabled in Government Cloud
When migration activities occur (data loads, user access, configuration changes)
Then all events are logged with timestamps, user IDs, IP addresses, and actions performed
And audit logs are retained for required compliance period (FedRAMP Moderate — 1 year online, 3 years archived)
And I can query audit logs to investigate data discrepancies or security incidents
And audit log exports are available for ISSO review and ATO reporting
source: SOO Section 6.5 — Platform Event Monitoring, FedRAMP audit requirements
assumptions: Platform Event Monitoring (part of Shield) configured for all high-risk events. Audit logs exported to SIEM or log management platform for long-term retention.

user_story: As a Contact Center Agent, I want to access a migration status dashboard during cutover so that I can provide accurate information to customers calling with questions about their reservations
acceptance_criteria:
Given cutover is in progress and customers may call with concerns
When I access the agent-facing migration status dashboard
Then I can see real-time status of each migration phase (completed, in-progress, not started)
And I can view known issues, workarounds, and customer-facing talking points
And I can access quick reference guides for common customer questions during transition
And dashboard updates automatically as migration progresses
And I have escalation contacts for issues I cannot resolve
source: SOO Section 6.6.12 — contact center operations, customer support during transition
assumptions: Service Cloud knowledge base or custom Lightning component used for status dashboard. Dashboard accessible to contact center agents via Service Cloud console.

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to reconcile migrated record counts and checksums against source data so that I can certify data completeness and integrity post-migration
acceptance_criteria:
Given migration has completed for a data entity
When I execute reconciliation scripts
Then record counts in Salesforce match source system counts within acceptable tolerance (e.g., <0.01% variance)
And checksums or hash totals for numeric fields (e.g., total revenue) match between systems
And 100% of critical records (active reservations, customer profiles) are accounted for
And discrepancies are investigated, documented, and resolved
And reconciliation report is signed off by business and technical owners
source: SOO Section 6.3 — data migration validation, quality assurance
assumptions: Reconciliation scripts compare aggregate counts and checksums. Sampling-based detailed comparison performed for high-risk data. Variance thresholds defined in migration plan.

user_story: As a Developer, I want to implement accessibility compliance for all migration tools and validation interfaces so that Section 508/WCAG Level AA requirements are met even for internal admin tools
acceptance_criteria:
Given Section 508/WCAG Level AA is required for all system interfaces
When I build custom migration dashboards, validation UIs, or admin tools
Then all interfaces meet accessibility standards (keyboard navigation, screen reader compatibility, color contrast)
And automated accessibility testing (e.g., axe DevTools) passes with zero violations
And manual accessibility testing confirms usability for users with disabilities
And accessibility compliance is documented in migration deliverables
source: SOO Section 6.6 — Section 508/WCAG Level AA accessibility requirements
assumptions: Lightning Web Components and standard Salesforce UI components used where possible for built-in accessibility. Custom components tested with accessibility tools and assistive technologies.

user_story: As a System Admin, I want to conduct post-migration hypercare support for 30 days so that any data issues, user access problems, or functional gaps are quickly identified and resolved
acceptance_criteria:
Given the system has gone live with migrated data
When hypercare support is active for the first 30 days post-cutover
Then dedicated support team monitors system performance, error logs, and user feedback daily
And high-priority data issues are resolved within 4 hours, medium within 1 business day
And user access and authentication problems are escalated immediately
And daily status reports are provided to stakeholders
And lessons learned are documented for future releases or system enhancements
source: SOO Section 6.2 — post-implementation support, SOO Section 6.3 — transition support
assumptions: Hypercare team includes developers, admins, and subject matter experts. 24/7 on-call rotation for critical issues during hypercare period. Transition to standard support model after hypercare ends.

user_story: As an Agency Program Manager, I want to view real-time reservation utilization dashboards in CRM Analytics so that I can monitor facility performance and make data-driven decisions about capacity planning.
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am logged into the Agency Portal with Program Manager role
When I navigate to the Analytics dashboard
Then I see facility utilization metrics (occupancy rate, revenue per available night, booking lead time) for my assigned facilities
And the data refreshes within 15 minutes of transaction completion
And I can filter by date range, facility type, and geographic region
And the dashboard meets Section 508/WCAG Level AA accessibility standards
source: SOO Section 6.7.8 - reporting requirements, SOO Section 6.6 - stakeholder tools
assumptions: CRM Analytics (Tableau) with live connection to Salesforce Platform objects (Reservation, Facility, Transaction). Data refresh via Platform Events or scheduled dataflows. Row-level security enforced by agency assignment field. Accessible visualizations using Tableau semantic roles and alt-text.

user_story: As a PMO Executive, I want to access monthly QASP performance metric reports in CRM Analytics so that I can verify contractor compliance and submit quality assurance surveillance reports to USDA leadership.
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am logged into the PMO dashboard
When I select the QASP Metrics report for a specific month
Then I see performance indicators (system availability 99.5%, reservation completion rate, average response time, customer satisfaction score)
And the report includes trend analysis comparing current month to prior 3 months
And I can export the report to PDF with USDA branding for official submission
And the report data is certified accurate and matches source system records
source: SOO Section 6.7.8 - monthly QASP reporting requirements
assumptions: CRM Analytics dashboard with embedded Tableau report. Data aggregated from Platform Event Monitoring (availability, performance), Service Cloud (CSAT), and Reservation objects. PDF export via CRM Analytics Download API with custom report template. Scheduled monthly snapshot dataset for historical comparisons.

user_story: As an Agency Program Manager, I want to run ad-hoc queries on reservation trends so that I can analyze seasonal demand patterns and optimize business rules for my facilities.
acceptance_criteria:
Given I have access to CRM Analytics Explorer
When I create a custom query filtering by facility, date range, and inventory type
Then I see results showing booking volume, cancellation rate, average length of stay, and revenue
And I can pivot data by day of week, booking channel (web/mobile/contact center), and customer segment

Manual testing by certified accessibility specialist. Remediation tracked in Salesforce Work Items with Section 508 tag.
