source: SOO Section 6.2.13 (training for concessionaires) + SOO Section 6.6 (concessionaire portal access)
assumptions: Experience Cloud partner portal with custom training compliance object tracking user, training module, completion date, and expiration date. Process Builder or Flow to trigger email reminders and portal restrictions. Shield encryption for concessionaire compliance data.

user_story: As a System Administrator, I want to author and publish training content using Salesforce Knowledge so that I can maintain up-to-date documentation for all user personas without requiring developer support.
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am a System Admin with content authorship permissions
When I create a new Knowledge article in Lightning Knowledge
Then I can use the rich text editor to add formatted text, images, videos, and hyperlinks
And I can assign data categories (persona: Field Staff, topic: Business Rules) for targeted content delivery
And I can preview the article in desktop and mobile views before publishing
And I can set the article visibility (Public, Internal, Partner) and publish to the appropriate channels
And I can track article views, feedback scores, and search rankings via reporting
source: SOO Section 6.2.13 (system documentation) + SOO Section 6.2.4 (admin tools)
assumptions: Salesforce Knowledge with Lightning Knowledge authoring interface. Data categories for persona-based and topic-based content organization. Knowledge metrics tracked via standard reports and CRM Analytics dashboards.

user_story: As a Program Manager in the PMO, I want to track sprint progress using DevOps Center so that I can monitor work item completion, code deployments, and release readiness across all environments.
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am overseeing a bi-weekly sprint
When I log into DevOps Center
Then I see a dashboard showing: active work items by status (In Progress, Code Review, Testing, Done), pipeline health for all environments (Dev, QA, UAT, Staging, Production), and deployment success rate
And I can drill into individual work items to view linked commits, pull requests, and test results
And I can trigger a deployment to UAT or Production via the GUI with approval gates
And the system tracks deployment history with timestamps, deployer identity, and change sets
And I receive Slack/email notifications for deployment failures or approval requests
source: SOO Section 6.2.4 (DevOps Center for CI/CD) + SOO Section 6.2.5 (Agile program management)
assumptions: DevOps Center configured with Git-based source control (GitHub Enterprise or Bitbucket). Work items tracked in DevOps Center or external Agile tool (Jira) with bi-directional sync. Approval processes configured via Salesforce Approval Process or DevOps Center native approvals. Slack/email notifications via Flow or Process Builder.

user_story: As a Developer, I want to commit code changes to a feature branch in Git and have them automatically deployed to a scratch org so that I can test changes in an isolated environment before merging to main.
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am working on a new feature (e.g., lottery booking logic)
When I push commits to a feature branch in GitHub
Then DevOps Center automatically triggers a CI pipeline that: validates syntax, runs Apex unit tests (80% minimum code coverage), deploys to a scratch org, and posts results to the pull request
And I can access the scratch org URL from the DevOps Center dashboard or GitHub PR comments
And the scratch org is pre-seeded with test data for the lottery booking scenario
And the scratch org expires after 7 days to conserve resources
And I can manually trigger a rebuild if the pipeline fails
source: SOO Section 6.2.4 (DevOps Center + CI/CD) + SOO Section 6.2.6 (developer sandbox environments)
assumptions: DevOps Center with GitHub Actions or Salesforce DX CLI integration for CI/CD. Scratch orgs provisioned via DevOps Center or Salesforce CLI with project scratch-def.json. Test data seeded via Apex scripts or data loader CSVs. Code coverage enforced via Apex test execution in CI pipeline.

user_story: As a Program Manager, I want to conduct quarterly Program Management Reviews (PMRs) with stakeholders using CRM Analytics dashboards so that I can present key performance metrics and demonstrate progress against QASP objectives.
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am preparing for a quarterly PMR with USDA stakeholders
When I access the PMO CRM Analytics dashboard
Then I see metrics for: Sprint velocity (story points completed per sprint), Defect density (bugs per 1,000 lines of code), Deployment frequency (releases per month), QASP compliance score (percentage of SLO metrics met), and User adoption (monthly active users by persona)
And I can filter data by quarter, epic, and agency
And I can export the dashboard as a PDF or PowerPoint slide for inclusion in the PMR deck
And the dashboard includes trend lines showing quarter-over-quarter improvement
And the data refreshes nightly via scheduled CRM Analytics dataflows
source: SOO Section 6.2.5 (Agile program management + quarterly PMRs) + SOO Section 6.7.8 (QASP-based surveillance)
assumptions: CRM Analytics (Tableau CRM) dashboard with custom datasets sourced from DevOps Center work items, Shield Event Monitoring (user adoption), and custom QASP metrics object. Scheduled dataflows refresh nightly. Export to PDF/PowerPoint via CRM Analytics native export or Einstein Analytics Tableau integration.

user_story: As a QA Tester, I want to execute UAT test scripts in a dedicated UAT environment and log defects directly in DevOps Center so that defects are tracked alongside development work items.
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am testing the lottery booking feature in the UAT environment
When I discover a defect (e.g., lottery results email not sending)
Then I can create a new work item in DevOps Center with type 'Bug', priority, severity, steps to reproduce, and screenshots
And the bug is automatically linked to the original user story (US-0401) via the epic/feature hierarchy
And the development team receives a Slack notification of the new bug
And I can track the bug status (Triaged, In Progress, Fixed, Verified) in DevOps Center
And the bug cannot be closed until I verify the fix in UAT and mark it 'Verified'
And the system tracks UAT pass/fail rate for QASP reporting
source: SOO Section 6.2.5 (UAT coordination) + SOO Section 6.2.4 (DevOps Center work item tracking)
assumptions: DevOps Center configured with custom work item types (Bug, User Story, Epic). UAT environment provisioned as a sandbox with production-like data (anonymized via Shield Data Mask). Slack integration via Flow or DevOps Center webhooks. QASP metrics tracked via custom reporting object synced from DevOps Center.

user_story: As a System Administrator, I want to generate comprehensive system documentation (data dictionary, object relationships, process flows) using Salesforce metadata so that I can maintain accurate documentation without manual effort.
acceptance_criteria:
Given I need to produce an updated data dictionary for the quarterly PMR
When I run the documentation generator tool (e.g., Salesforce Inspector, Schema Lister, or custom Apex script)
Then the system produces a report listing all custom objects, fields (API name, label, data type, required, help text, picklist values), validation rules, and relationships (lookup, master-detail)
And the report includes process flows exported from Flow Builder as PDF diagrams
And the report includes Apex class annotations and code comments for custom business logic
And the output is formatted as a Word document or Confluence page with a table of contents
And the documentation includes versioning metadata (last modified date, author, release number)
source: SOO Section 6.2 (system documentation) + SOO Section 6.2.6 (code annotations)
assumptions: Salesforce Metadata API accessed via Salesforce Inspector, Schema Lister app, or custom Apex REST API. Flow Builder exports via Metadata API or manual PDF export. Code annotations maintained in Apex classes via JavaDoc-style comments. Documentation generator outputs to Word via Salesforce Files or Confluence via REST API integration.

user_story: As a Technical Architect, I want to maintain a solution architecture diagram with annotations for FedRAMP controls so that I can demonstrate compliance mappings during the ATO audit.
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am preparing for the annual FedRAMP audit
When I access the architecture documentation in Confluence or Salesforce Files
Then I see a current solution architecture diagram (Lucidchart, Visio, or Miro) showing: Salesforce Government Cloud, Experience Cloud portals, Data Cloud, Shield encryption, Login.gov SSO, external integrations (Worldpay, US Bank Lockbox), and third-party APIs (Mapbox, ArcGIS)
And each component is annotated with relevant FedRAMP controls (e.g., 'AC-2: Account Management via Login.gov SSO', 'SC-13: Cryptographic Protection via Shield Platform Encryption')
And the diagram includes data flow arrows showing PII handling and encryption at rest/in transit
And the diagram is versioned with a change log and stored in the ATO documentation package
And the diagram is updated quarterly and reviewed by the ISSM/ISSO
source: SOO Section 6.4 (FedRAMP Moderate compliance + ATO) + SOO Section 6.2 (architecture documentation)
assumptions: Architecture diagrams maintained in Lucidchart, Visio, or Miro with FedRAMP control annotations. Diagrams exported as PDF/PNG and stored in Salesforce Files or Confluence. ATO documentation package includes architecture diagrams, security plan, and control traceability matrix. Quarterly review process enforced via PMO process.

user_story: As a Product Owner, I want to prioritize the product backlog in DevOps Center based on stakeholder feedback and QASP performance metrics so that I can maximize value delivery in each sprint.
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am planning Sprint 15
When I access the DevOps Center backlog view
Then I see all unscheduled work items (user stories, bugs, technical debt) ranked by priority (High, Medium, Low)
And I can drag-and-drop work items to reorder the backlog
And I can filter by epic, persona, or agency to focus on specific themes
And I can view QASP metrics (e.g., 'Reservation success rate: 98.5% — target: 99%') to identify high-priority defects
And I can assign work items to the upcoming sprint with a target commitment of 40 story points
And the team can view the sprint backlog in DevOps Center and track daily progress
source: SOO Section 6.2.5 (Agile program management + sprint planning) + SOO Section 6.7.8 (QASP-based prioritization)
assumptions: DevOps Center configured with backlog management, sprint planning, and story point estimation. QASP metrics synced from CRM Analytics or custom reporting objects. Backlog prioritization based on MoSCoW method (Must, Should, Could, Won't). Story points estimated via Planning Poker or async estimation in DevOps Center.

user_story: As a Scrum Master, I want to facilitate daily standups and retrospectives using DevOps Center and Miro so that the team can collaborate effectively in a hybrid (remote + on-site) environment.
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am facilitating a daily standup for a distributed team
When I open the DevOps Center sprint board
Then I can view all in-progress work items with assignees, status, and blockers
And I can update work item status (In Progress → Code Review → Done) via drag-and-drop
And the team can add comments to work items to document blockers or questions
And after the sprint ends, I can export sprint metrics (velocity, burndown chart) for the retrospective
And I can facilitate the retrospective in Miro with virtual sticky notes for 'What went well', 'What didn't go well', and 'Action items'
And action items are captured as new work items in DevOps Center for the next sprint
source: SOO Section 6.2.5 (Agile program management + sprint ceremonies)
assumptions: DevOps Center sprint board with Kanban-style columns (To Do, In Progress, Code Review, Testing, Done). Burndown charts generated via DevOps Center or CRM Analytics. Retrospective facilitation in Miro or Mural with integration to DevOps Center via Zapier or custom API. Hybrid team collaboration via Zoom, Slack, and Salesforce Chatter.

user_story: As a Developer, I want to annotate Apex classes with inline code comments following JavaDoc standards so that future maintainers can understand the business logic without tribal knowledge.
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am writing a new Apex class for lottery selection logic
When I create methods in the class
Then I include JavaDoc-style comments above each method with: /** @description [what the method does], @param [parameter descriptions], @return [return value description], @throws [exception descriptions] */
And I include inline comments for complex logic blocks (e.g., weighted lottery algorithm)
And the code comments reference the originating user story (US-0401) for traceability
And the code passes the team's code review checklist (minimum 80% coverage, comments for all public methods, no hardcoded values)
And the documentation generator tool can parse the JavaDoc comments to produce API documentation
source: SOO Section 6.2.6 (code annotations) + SOO Section 6.2.4 (maintainability standards)
assumptions: Apex code annotations follow JavaDoc convention. Code review enforced via pull request reviews in GitHub with required approvals. Code coverage tracked via Salesforce Apex test execution (minimum 80% per SOO Section 6.2.4). Documentation generator uses Salesforce Metadata API or ApexDoc tool to extract comments.

user_story: As a Release Manager, I want to execute a production deployment using DevOps Center with automated rollback capability so that I can minimize downtime and risk during releases.
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am deploying Release 2.5 to Production
When I trigger the deployment from DevOps Center
Then the system executes a pre-deployment checklist: backup current metadata, validate change set in Production (checkOnly), run all Apex tests (100% of classes), and verify QASP SLO thresholds are met
And the deployment executes during the approved maintenance window (Sunday 2-6am ET) with stakeholder notification
And if the deployment fails or QASP metrics degrade by >5%, the system automatically triggers a rollback to the previous release
And the rollback completes within 30 minutes with automated notification to the PMO
And all deployment events are logged to Shield Event Monitoring for audit trails
And I can view deployment status in real-time via the DevOps Center dashboard
source: SOO Section 6.2.4 (DevOps Center CI/CD + rollback capability) + SOO Section 6.7.8 (QASP-based release gates)
assumptions: DevOps Center configured with automated deployment pipelines to Production. Pre-deployment validation via Salesforce Metadata API (checkOnly=true). Automated rollback via Git revert + DevOps Center redeploy of previous commit. QASP metrics monitored via CRM Analytics API or custom Apex callout. Shield Event Monitoring logs all deployment events with transaction security policies.

user_story: As an Agency Program Manager, I want to receive quarterly training effectiveness reports showing completion rates and assessment scores for my field staff so that I can identify knowledge gaps and coaching opportunities.
acceptance_criteria:
Given I manage 150 field staff across 30 campgrounds
When I access the training dashboard in CRM Analytics
Then I see a report showing: total training hours completed, completion rate by module (Inventory Management: 95%, Business Rules: 78%, Reporting: 82%), average assessment scores, and time-to-completion
And I can drill down to individual staff members to view their training transcript
And I can identify staff who have not completed required training by the deadline
And I can export the report as a CSV for inclusion in quarterly performance reviews
And I receive automated email alerts when completion rates drop below 85%
source: SOO Section 6.2.13 (training effectiveness tracking) + SOO Section 6.7.8 (reporting requirements)
assumptions: Training completion tracked via custom Training object with fields: User, Module, Completion Date, Assessment Score, and Status. CRM Analytics dashboard with datasets sourced from Training object. Email alerts triggered via Flow or Process Builder when completion rate formula field drops below threshold. CSV export via CRM Analytics native export.

user_story: As a Public User with a visual impairment, I want to access training videos with audio descriptions and keyboard-navigable transcripts so that I can learn how to use the reservation system independently.
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am a screen reader user accessing the Help section
When I navigate to a training video on 'How to Book a Campsite'
Then the video includes synchronized closed captions and an audio description track describing visual elements
And I can access a full text transcript below the video that is keyboard-navigable and screen reader compatible
And the video player controls (play, pause, volume, speed) are operable via keyboard (tab, enter, space)
And the page meets WCAG 2.1 Level AA success criteria (1.2.3: Audio Description, 1.2.5: Audio Description Prerecorded, 2.1.1: Keyboard)
And I can adjust playback speed (0.5x, 1x, 1.5x, 2x) to accommodate my learning preferences
source: SOO Section 6.5.3 (Section 508/WCAG 2.1 Level AA compliance) + SOO Section 6.2.13 (public user training)
assumptions: Training videos hosted in Salesforce CMS or external provider (YouTube, Vimeo) with Section 508-compliant media player. Closed captions and audio descriptions produced per WCAG 1.2.3/1.2.5 requirements. Transcripts stored as HTML with semantic markup (headings, lists) for screen reader compatibility. Video player is keyboard-accessible per WCAG 2.1.1.

user_story: As a System Administrator, I want to schedule automated Knowledge article reviews every 6 months so that outdated content is flagged for revision and accuracy is maintained.
acceptance_criteria:
Given I have 500+ Knowledge articles published across multiple topics
When an article reaches its 6-month anniversary from the last update
Then the system automatically flags the article with status 'Review Required' and assigns it to the original author or designated subject matter expert
And the assignee receives an email notification with a link to the article and a review checklist
And the article is temporarily hidden from public search results until the review is completed
And the reviewer can mark the article as 'Current' (no changes), 'Updated' (revised content), or 'Archived' (no longer relevant)
And the system tracks article review history (review date, reviewer, outcome) for audit purposes
And I can view a dashboard showing articles overdue for review
source: SOO Section 6.2.13 (knowledge base maintenance) + SOO Section 6.2 (documentation accuracy)
assumptions: Salesforce Knowledge with custom date formula fields (Last Updated Date + 180 days = Review Due Date). Scheduled Flow runs daily to identify articles due for review and assign tasks. Email notifications via Flow-triggered email templates. Article visibility controlled via Knowledge publication status or custom 'Review Status' picklist. Review history tracked via custom Review History related object.

user_story: As a Training Administrator, I want to create interactive training assessments with branching logic so that learners receive personalized feedback based on their responses.
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am creating an assessment for the 'Business Rules Configuration' training module
When I build the assessment in the LMS or Survey tool
Then I can add multiple question types: multiple choice, true/false, scenario-based (case study), and open-ended
And I can configure branching logic: if learner answers Q3 incorrectly, they are routed to a remedial module before continuing
And I can provide immediate feedback for each question with explanations for correct/incorrect answers
And the assessment requires a minimum score of 80% to pass, with unlimited retake attempts
And the system tracks attempt history (date, score, time spent) for each learner
And I can export assessment results to CSV for analysis
source: SOO Section 6.2.13 (training assessment and effectiveness)
assumptions: Training assessments built using Salesforce Surveys, Experience Cloud forms with custom Apex logic, or third-party LMS integration (e.g., myTrailhead, Cornerstone). Branching logic implemented via conditional visibility rules or Apex controller. Assessment results stored in custom Assessment object with fields: User, Module, Attempt Number, Score, Completion Date. CSV export via Data Loader or custom Apex REST API.

user_story: As a Program Manager, I want to monitor DevOps Center pipeline performance metrics (build success rate, deployment frequency, lead time) so that I can identify bottlenecks and optimize the CI/CD process.
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am reviewing DevOps maturity for the quarterly PMR
When I access the DevOps metrics dashboard in CRM Analytics
Then I see metrics aligned with DORA (DevOps Research and Assessment) standards: Deployment Frequency (deploys per week), Lead Time for Changes (commit to production), Mean Time to Recovery (MTTR for incidents), and Change Failure Rate (percentage of deployments causing incidents)
And I can view trend lines over the past 12 months to track improvement
And I can filter by environment (Dev, QA, UAT, Production) and team
And the dashboard highlights underperforming metrics in red (e.g., 'Lead Time: 14 days — target: <7 days')
And I can drill into specific deployments to identify root causes of delays or failures
source: SOO Section 6.2.4 (DevOps Center CI/CD optimization) + SOO Section 6.2.5 (program performance metrics)
assumptions: DevOps Center deployment data exported to CRM Analytics via Salesforce Connect or custom ETL. DORA metrics calculated via CRM Analytics SAQL or Apex scheduled batch job. Incident data sourced from Service Cloud cases or custom Incident object. Dashboard includes drill-down capability via CRM Analytics faceting and linked dashboards.

user_story: As an Information System Security Officer (ISSO), I want to generate quarterly security training compliance reports showing completion rates for mandatory FedRAMP awareness training so that I can demonstrate compliance during the ATO audit.
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am preparing for the annual FedRAMP audit
When I access the security training compliance dashboard
Then I see a report showing: total users (government, field, concessionaire), completion rate for FedRAMP Awareness Training (target: 100%), completion rate for Incident Response Training (target: 100% for admins), and completion rate for PII Handling Training (target: 100% for contact center agents)
And I can identify users who have not completed required training with details (name, role, last login date)
And I can export the report as a PDF for inclusion in the ATO evidence package
And the system tracks training certificates with digital signatures and completion timestamps
And I can demonstrate continuous monitoring via quarterly compliance trend charts
source: SOO Section 6.4 (FedRAMP Moderate compliance + ATO) + SOO Section 6.2.13 (mandatory security training)
assumptions: Security training modules hosted in LMS or Salesforce Knowledge with mandatory completion requirements. Completion tracked via custom Security Training object with fields: User, Training Type, Completion Date, Certificate URL. Compliance dashboard built in CRM Analytics with datasets sourced from Security Training object. Digital signatures via DocuSign or Salesforce eSignature. Quarterly trend analysis via CRM Analytics date grouping.

user_story: As a Technical Writer, I want to maintain user guides and API documentation in Confluence with version control so that I can track changes and roll back to previous versions if needed.
acceptance_criteria:
Given I am updating the 'Agency Program Manager User Guide' for Release 2.5
When I edit the Confluence page
Then I can add/modify content using the rich text editor with support for: formatted text, tables, embedded images, Lucidchart diagrams, and code blocks
And I can add a version comment describing the changes (e.g., 'Added section on lottery configuration')
And the system automatically tracks version history with timestamps and author names
And I can compare any two versions side-by-side to see differences highlighted
And I can roll back to a previous version if the current changes need to be reverted
And I can restrict page editing to authorized users (Technical Writers, System Admins) while allowing read access to all users
And I can export the user guide as a PDF with auto-generated table of contents
source: SOO Section 6.2 (system documentation + user guides) + SOO Section 6.2.4 (version control)
assumptions: Confluence Cloud or Data Center with version history enabled. User guides organized in Confluence spaces by persona (Agency Program Manager, Field Staff, Public User). Lucidchart, Miro, or Visio diagrams embedded via Confluence app integrations. PDF export via Confluence native export with auto-generated TOC. Access controls via Confluence page restrictions and space permissions.
